chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No. D-2151 of 2018 (Jamaluddin & others v. P.O Sindh & others) Present:- Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro & Mr. Justice Arbab Ali Hakro Mr. Bilawal Hussain, Advocate for the petitioners. Mr. Ali Raza Baloch, Assistant A.G – Sindh a/w Aijaz Hussain Rajput, Assistant Director, STEVTA, Sukkur. Date of Hearing & Decision: 22-11-2023 O R D E R MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO, J:- Petitioners claim to be daily wages employees working under respondents No.3&4 i.e. Vice Chancellor, Benazir Bhutto Shaheed University, Khairpur and Principal, Government College of Technology, Khairpur. They have filed this petition for regularization of their services and release of salaries. 2. Learned counsel for petitioners submits that initially the petitioners were hired by Government College of Technology, Khairpur and thereafter their services were transferred to Sindh Technical Education & Vocational Training Authority (STEVTA). However, he has failed to show any document showing that either the petitioners were hired by the College and transferred to STEVTA or vice versa. Lastly, learned counsel submits that the petitioners have not been paid their salaries either. However, no material has been referred by him to show for what period the petitioners worked and where so that salaries are ordered to be released. 3. The Secretary, Universities and Boards Department has filed a compliance report stating that on 04.07.2022, the petitioners were given an opportunity to appear before the Chairman, Scrutiny Committee, where they admitted that they were appointed for six months on contract basis and their contract was never extended.

However, no material has been referred by him to show for what period the petitioners worked and where so that salaries are ordered to be released. 3. The Secretary, Universities and Boards Department has filed a compliance report stating that on 04.07.2022, the petitioners were given an opportunity to appear before the Chairman, Scrutiny Committee, where they admitted that they were appointed for six months on contract basis and their contract was never extended. Therefore, no case of their regularization is made out. Cost. Petition No.D-2151 of 2018 Page 2 of 2 4. We in the light of such comments and looking at the material available on record are of the same view. Petitioners have no case to agitate in the constitutional jurisdiction of this Court. Accordingly, this petition along with pending application(s) is dismissed. However, the petitioners would be at liberty to move proper forum for release of their salaries, if any, outstanding against any of the respondents. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmad
